system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

maxDeltaT       0.002;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           1;

ddtSchemes
{

}

{

}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{

    reconstruct(rho) VanAlbada;

}

snGradSchemes
{

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.2;
        e               0.3;
    }
    equations
    {
        rho       0.3;
        rhoUx     0.5;
        rhoUy     0.5;
        rhoE      0.3;
    }
}

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
Unknown discretisation scheme VanAlbada

Valid schemes are :
58
(
CoBlended
Gamma
Gamma01
LUST
MUSCL
MUSCL01
Minmod
OSPRE
QUICK
SFCD
SuperBee
UMIST
biLinearFit
blended
cellCoBlended
clippedLinear
cubic
cubicUpwindFit
deferred
downwind
filteredLinear
filteredLinear2
filteredLinear3
fixedBlended
harmonic
limitWith
limitedCubic
limitedCubic01
limitedGamma
limitedLimitedCubic
limitedLimitedLinear
limitedLinear
limitedLinear01
limitedMUSCL
limitedVanLeer
limiterBlended
linear
linearFit
linearPureUpwindFit
linearUpwind
localBlended
localMax
localMin
midPoint
outletStabilised
phaseStabilised
pointLinear
quadraticFit
quadraticLinearFit
quadraticLinearUpwindFit
quadraticUpwindFit
reverseLinear
skewCorrected
upwind
vanAlbada
vanLeer
vanLeer01
weighted
)

file: .../system/fvSchemes/interpolationSchemes/reconstruct(rho) at line 34.
```

The `VanAlbada` limiter is **not listed** as a valid reconstruction scheme in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). Looking at the valid schemes list, `vanAlbada` (without capital A) is indeed listed at the bottom, but `VanAlbada` (capital V) is not. The error message shows `vanAlbada` in lowercase as a valid option, but the configuration file uses `VanAlbada` with a capital V.

## Resolution

### Change `reconstruct(rho)` from `VanAlbada` to `vanAlbada` in `system/fvSchemes`

In the `system/fvSchemes` file, change:
```
reconstruct(rho) VanAlbada;
```
to:
```
reconstruct(rho) vanAlbada;
```

Similarly, check if `reconstruct(T)` also uses `VanAlbada` and change it to `vanAlbada` as well.

The key principle is to use the **exact lowercase spelling** of the limiter name as shown in the error message's list of valid schemes. In OpenFOAM 10, the reconstruction limiters are all in lowercase (e.g., `minmod`, `vanleer`, `vanalbada`), not capitalized. The capitalization from previous attempts is what causes this error.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Change reconstruct(rho) from VanAlbada to vanAlbada; Change reconstruct(T) from VanAlbada to vanAlbada'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 1.1m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
